---

# UC-13.7.10 · Fluent Bit Multiline Parser Stuck-State Detection, Incomplete-Record Forensics, Stack-Trace Truncation, and Per-Parser Buffer-Timeout Misconfiguration Audit

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the glue that reassembles long error messages from many short lines. When that glue stops at the wrong line, messages arrive chopped off or mashed together. We catch that early so teams fix the parsing recipe before support wastes days on fake stack traces.*

---

## Description

Detects Fluent Bit multiline parser stuck-state and merge failures by correlating Prometheus-style ratios of fluentbit_multiline_emit_total to fluentbit_input_records_total, timeout-aligned partial emissions, structured multiline server logs, configuration snapshots that reveal declared versus runtime parser drift, downstream Java stack truncation cliffs in Splunk, cross-datacenter baseline divergence from inventory lookups, and supportive Splunk Common Information Model Performance host buckets—without conflating the pattern with generic buffer overflow, output retry exhaustion, or Transport Layer Security faults.

## Value

Platform and application reliability teams shorten mean time to innocence when incidents look like code regressions yet the root cause is a silent multiline rule that never reaches end-of-record, because merged stacks arrive truncated or stitched incorrectly long before queues overflow. Risk and engineering governance groups receive a tamper-resistant narrative that separates intentional low-latency flush_timeout policies from misconfiguration, highlights fleet Fluent Bit version skew, and preserves evidence for post-incident reviews.

## Implementation

Land fluentbit:metrics on otel_metrics, fluentbit:server_log multiline diagnostics on ops, app:java downstream validation on app, fluentbit:config_snapshot exports, and accelerated Performance summaries; maintain fluentbit_pod_inventory.csv and fluentbit_multiline_parser_inventory.csv; schedule observability_uc_13_7_10_fluentbit_multiline_stuck_state every fifteen minutes with earliest=-6h@h; route critical and high severities to platform logging on-call; archive weekly evidence exports.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_7_10_fluentbit_multiline_stuck_state with fifteen minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; alert routing to Head of Platform delegated on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab logging namespace, deploy a multiline rule with an end regular expression that cannot match sample Java stack lines, confirm otel_metrics emit ratios fall against baseline, fluentbit:server_log multiline lines report timeout language, app:java fixtures show stack_truncated spikes, and observability_uc_13_7_10_fluentbit_multiline_stuck_state returns critical or high within thirty minutes.

### Negative scenario

After restoring the correct end pattern, rolling the DaemonSet, and validating ratios return to inventory expected_emit_ratio_baseline with quiet multiline server logs, confirm severity returns to low or medium noise floor and filtering to critical plus high yields zero rows for thirty consecutive scheduled runs.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability pipeline team that operates Fluent Bit DaemonSets, sidecars, and bare-metal agents, the Kubernetes platform group that rolls logging Helm charts, application reliability engineers who own Java and Python stack-trace formats, and the Splunk administrators who index OpenTelemetry metrics and application logs. This use case isolates Fluent Bit multiline parser stuck-state behavior: when a first-line or continuation regular expression no longer matches production log lines, partial records linger inside the engine buffer until flush_timeout_ms forces emission or an unrelated line accidentally satisfies the end rule. The symptom class is distinct from generic buffer overflow (UC-13.7.1), output retry storms (UC-13.7.2), raw I/O throughput collapse (UC-13.7.3), worker process death (UC-13.7.4), memory buffer proximity (UC-13.7.5), end-to-end flush latency (UC-13.7.6), filter-driven drops (UC-13.7.7), filesystem storage backlog (UC-13.7.8), and Transport Layer Security handshake failures (UC-13.7.9). Here the failure mode is semantic: the state machine for multiline merge never reaches a clean record boundary, so operators see ratio collapse between fluentbit_multiline_emit_total and fluentbit_input_records_total, timed emissions aligned to flush_timeout_ms, truncated stack traces at Splunk search time, and configuration drift between declared multiline_parser names and the parser section the runtime actually applied after a partial reload.

Understanding Fluent Bit multiline requires separating docker JSON, CRI partial-tag, and custom regular-expression modes. Docker logs often arrive as single-line JSON envelopes; multiline filters unwrap stack bodies. CRI pipelines depend on the P, F, partial flags in the tag; mis-tuned parsers that assume Docker semantics on CRI inputs stall because continuation lines never arrive in the shape the rule expects. Regular-expression multiline rules pair a flush_timeout_ms with start and end patterns; when application logging adds a vendor prefix or changes indentation, the end pattern may never fire, so emit totals stall while input counters climb. Older Fluent Bit minor releases shipped edge-case bugs in multiline regular-expression handling; fleet version skew therefore matters and belongs in inventory lookups alongside expected baseline ratios.

Splunk-side forensics complement agent metrics. Downstream sourcetype=app:java events may carry multilineerror flags or truncated tails inserted by forwarders when line-breaking rules fight the merged record. Measuring stack_lines_count distributions per cluster establishes language-specific baselines; cliff drops after lines such as at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0 often indicate the merge gave up mid-trace. Cross-datacenter comparison of truncation rates on logically identical workloads almost always traces to configuration drift rather than application regression. Hybrid estates that still run Fluentd on some nodes can mirror concat filter behavior; this card cites Fluentd documentation for operators bridging pipelines but keeps primary detection on Fluent Bit telemetry lanes.

Risk briefing: canary pods and deploy storms emit short bursts of single-line messages before the first real exception arrives, which temporarily depresses emit ratios without proving a stuck parser. Intentionally aggressive flush_timeout_ms values for low-latency tracing pipelines will show timeout-driven emissions that are policy-approved, not incidents. Legal reviewers should confirm whether raw stack traces in evidence indexes meet data-handling policy; redact customer payloads in pager templates while retaining full raw for a break-glass role.

RBAC and safety: scrapers that read index=otel_metrics must use read-only service identities. Config snapshot jobs that export fluentbit:config_snapshot need narrowly scoped Kubernetes read access to ConfigMaps and Secrets that hold parser fragments, not cluster-admin interactive credentials on laptops. Change windows should stamp change_ticket_id onto snapshot events so Splunk timelines align with approved rollouts.

Differentiation recap: multiline parser stuck-state, flush-timeout ejection signatures, per-parser emit ratio anomalies, declared versus runtime parser drift, stack-trace truncation forensics, Fluent Bit version skew from inventory, downstream broken-event sanity checks, and optional Fluentd concat context—not RAM buffer overflow, not generic flush latency percentiles, not TLS errors, not filter drop math in isolation.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms Splunk can fuse without assuming a single exporter always works during incidents.

OpenTelemetry metrics arm: scrape the Fluent Bit Prometheus exporter or OpenTelemetry Collector receiver into index=otel_metrics with sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics. Preserve labels that map to fluentbit_input_id, fluentbit_output_id, multiline_parser, pod_name, host, and cluster. Required series include fluentbit_input_records_total, fluentbit_input_bytes_total, and fluentbit_multiline_emit_total when your build exposes them; alias vendor-specific names into these fields at ingest. Record flush_timeout_ms when exposed as a label or parallel gauge; otherwise join from config snapshots.

Structured multiline diagnostics arm: enable elevated server logging for the multiline component on canary pods first, then production when governance approves. Land lines in index=ops with sourcetype=fluentbit:server_log and field component=multiline. Capture buffer-stuck language, partial flush reasons, and parser identification tokens. Strip ANSI color, normalize UTC timestamps, and map kubernetes.pod_name into pod_name for stable joins.

Downstream application validation arm: forward Splunk-side application logs into index=app with sourcetype=app:java for JVM stacks, and parallel sourcetypes for Python if your schema uses them. Extract stack_lines_count, stack_truncated, and multilineerror from props.conf FIELDALIAS and calculated fields. Ensure cluster and pod_name flow from Kubernetes metadata annotations.

Configuration snapshot arm: schedule a GitOps or in-cluster exporter that emits fluentbit:config_snapshot events into index=ops whenever DaemonSets roll. Each snapshot should include multiline_parser declarations, active parser names, pattern excerpts, flush_timeout_ms per rule, and Fluent Bit version strings. Hash canonical YAML without volatile resourceVersion when auditors need tamper hints.

CIM Performance correlation arm: accelerate Splunk Common Information Model Performance summaries for hosts that run Fluent Bit so tstats arms can quantify load anomalies that coincide with parser churn. This arm is supportive: high CPU on a node alone does not prove multiline issues, but paired with ratio collapse it prioritizes investigation order.

Maintain fluentbit_pod_inventory.csv with columns cluster, pod_name, role, owner, expected_baseline, owner_team_lookup, and sister_datacenter_label for cross-DC ratio comparisons. Maintain fluentbit_multiline_parser_inventory.csv with parser_id, cluster, pattern_type, expected_emit_ratio_baseline, sister_dc_ratio_floor, fluent_bit_min_version, and notes for docker versus cri versus regex modes. Refresh lookups weekly or automate from configuration management databases.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

```bash
kubectl -n logging logs daemonset/fluent-bit -c fluent-bit --tail=300 | rg -i "multiline|parser|timeout"
kubectl -n logging get configmap fluent-bit-config -o yaml | rg -i "multiline|flush_timeout"
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:2020/api/v1/metrics/prometheus | rg -i "multiline|fluentbit_input"
```

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE sufficiently large for merged stacks, LINE_BREAKER must not fragment records that multiline already merged, and FIELDALIAS maps should unify dotted versus flattened Prometheus labels.

Pre-save validation: each arm must return events in lab within one hour of enabling collection. Induce a harmless multiline mismatch in non-production, confirm otel_metrics ratios move, server_log multiline lines appear, app:java shows elevated stack_truncated in sample fixtures, config_snapshot captures the bad rule, and tstats returns host buckets.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_10_fluentbit_multiline_stuck_state with a fifteen minute schedule during steady-state production, dispatch earliest=-6h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after tuning for fleet noise. Throttle duplicate cluster, pod_name, multiline_parser tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies so incident commanders open runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Prometheus-style Fluent Bit metrics, structured multiline server logs, downstream Java truncation signals, immutable configuration snapshots, and Performance datamodel tstats so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine parser crisis. coalesce() ladders absorb field naming drift across exporters and Kubernetes labels. streamstats builds per-merge_key windows that approximate rolling emit behavior and time_since_last_emit_seconds staleness. eventstats adds per-cluster median emit_ratio and stack_lines_count baselines for cliff-drop math. case() maps combined signals into critical, high, medium, and low tiers. inputlookup joins enrich cluster and parser rows with expected_emit_ratio_baseline, sister_dc_ratio_floor, and fluent_bit_min_version for governance overlays.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.10 Fluent Bit multiline stuck-state, flush_timeout ejection, parser drift, stack-trace truncation; tunables earliest=-6h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=otel_metrics sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,eks_cluster,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,pod,kubernetes.pod_name,fluentbit_pod,"unknown_pod"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,kubernetes.node_name,node,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval multiline_parser=trim(toString(coalesce(multiline_parser,parser,mlp_name,"")))
      | eval parser=trim(toString(coalesce(parser,multiline_parser,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_input_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_input_id,input_name,plugin_id,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_output_id,output_name,"")))
      | eval inp_rec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_input_records_total,fb_input_records,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval inp_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_input_bytes_total,fb_input_bytes,"0")),10)
      | eval ml_emit=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_multiline_emit_total,fb_multiline_emit_total,"0")),10)
      | eval emit_ratio=if(inp_rec>0 AND ml_emit>=0, round(ml_emit/inp_rec,5), null())
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_timeout_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,rule_pattern,"")))
      | eval lane="otel_prom_fluentbit"
      | eval stack_lines_count=null()
      | eval stack_truncated=null()
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=null()
      | eval stuck_records_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count ]
    [ search index=ops sourcetype=fluentbit:server_log component=multiline earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,kubernetes.pod_name,host,"unknown_pod"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,kubernetes.node_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval multiline_parser=trim(toString(coalesce(multiline_parser,parser,mlp,"")))
      | eval parser=multiline_parser
      | eval fluentbit_input_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_input_id,input_id,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_output_id,"")))
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_timeout_ms,timeout_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,regex_tail,"")))
      | eval stuck_hint=if(match(lower(_raw),"timeout|flush|partial|buffer|stuck|incomplete"),1,0)
      | eval inp_rec=tonumber("0",10)
      | eval inp_bytes=tonumber("0",10)
      | eval ml_emit=tonumber("0",10)
      | eval emit_ratio=null()
      | eval lane="fluentbit_server_multiline_log"
      | eval stack_lines_count=null()
      | eval stack_truncated=null()
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(time_since_last_emit_seconds,idle_sec,"0")),10)
      | eval stuck_records_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(stuck_records_count,buffer_stuck,"0")),10)
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count stuck_hint ]
    [ search index=app sourcetype=app:java earliest=-6h@h latest=now multilineerror=*
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,dc,rack,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,k8s_pod,host,"unknown_pod"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval multiline_parser="downstream_splunk"
      | eval parser=multiline_parser
      | eval fluentbit_input_id="na_downstream"
      | eval fluentbit_output_id="na_downstream"
      | eval inp_rec=0
      | eval inp_bytes=0
      | eval ml_emit=0
      | eval emit_ratio=null()
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=0
      | eval pattern=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(_raw,""))),1,120)
      | eval stack_lines_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(stack_lines_count,stack_depth,lines_in_stack,"0")),10)
      | eval stack_truncated=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(stack_truncated,truncated,stack_trunc,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="app_java_downstream_truncation"
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=null()
      | eval stuck_records_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count ]
    [ search index=ops sourcetype=fluentbit:config_snapshot earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,host,"unknown_pod"))))
      | eval host=pod_name
      | eval multiline_parser=trim(toString(coalesce(multiline_parser,declared_mlp,"")))
      | eval parser=trim(toString(coalesce(parser,active_parser,runtime_parser,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_input_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_input_id,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_output_id,"")))
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_timeout_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,first_line_regex,"")))
      | eval cfg_drift=if(len(multiline_parser)>0 AND len(parser)>0 AND multiline_parser!=parser,1,0)
      | eval inp_rec=0
      | eval inp_bytes=0
      | eval ml_emit=0
      | eval emit_ratio=null()
      | eval lane="fluentbit_config_snapshot"
      | eval stack_lines_count=null()
      | eval stack_truncated=null()
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=null()
      | eval stuck_records_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count cfg_drift ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=false count AS perf_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-6h@h latest=now BY host
      | eval cluster="perf_datamodel_host"
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval host=pod_name
      | eval multiline_parser="perf_correlation"
      | eval parser=multiline_parser
      | eval fluentbit_input_id="perf_dm"
      | eval fluentbit_output_id="perf_dm"
      | eval inp_rec=perf_hits
      | eval inp_bytes=0
      | eval ml_emit=0
      | eval emit_ratio=null()
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=0
      | eval pattern="Performance.host_load_correlation"
      | eval lane="cim_performance_tstats"
      | eval stack_lines_count=null()
      | eval stack_truncated=null()
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=null()
      | eval stuck_records_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count ]
| eval merge_key=strcat(cluster,"|",pod_name,"|",coalesce(multiline_parser,"__none__"))
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=24 current=t global=f last(ml_emit) AS prev_ml last(inp_rec) AS prev_inp last(_time) AS prev_ts BY merge_key
| eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=coalesce(time_since_last_emit_seconds,if(isnotnull(prev_ts),round(_time-prev_ts,2),null()))
| eval emit_ratio_roll=if(isnotnull(emit_ratio),emit_ratio,if(prev_inp>0 AND prev_ml>=0,round(prev_ml/max(prev_inp,1),5),null())
| eventstats median(emit_ratio) AS dc_emit_p50 stdev(emit_ratio) AS dc_emit_sd BY cluster multiline_parser
| eventstats median(stack_lines_count) AS stack_p50 stdev(stack_lines_count) AS stack_sd BY cluster
| join type=left max=0 cluster pod_name
    [| inputlookup fluentbit_pod_inventory.csv
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(pod_name)))
     | fields cluster pod_name role owner expected_baseline owner_team_lookup ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster multiline_parser
    [| inputlookup fluentbit_multiline_parser_inventory.csv
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | eval multiline_parser=trim(toString(parser_id))
     | fields cluster multiline_parser pattern_type expected_emit_ratio_baseline sister_dc_ratio_floor fluent_bit_min_version ]
| eval baseline_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_emit_ratio_baseline,expected_baseline,"0.02")),10)
| eval ratio_gap=if(isnotnull(emit_ratio) AND baseline_ratio>0, round(emit_ratio/baseline_ratio,4), null())
| eval trunc_cliff=if(stack_truncated==1 AND isnotnull(stack_p50) AND stack_lines_count<stack_p50*0.35,1,0)
| eval severity=case(trunc_cliff==1 AND match(lower(pattern),"nativemethodaccessor|traceback|caused by"),"critical", ratio_gap>0 AND ratio_gap<0.2 AND inp_rec>500,"high", coalesce(cfg_drift,0)==1,"high", coalesce(stuck_records_count,0)>=3 OR time_since_last_emit_seconds>480,"high", stack_truncated==1,"medium", dc_emit_sd>0 AND emit_ratio<dc_emit_p50*0.5,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","rewire_multiline_rule_and_validate_java_python_tail_in_splunk_sample", severity=="high","diff_config_snapshot_reload_hot_and_compare_sister_dc_inventory", severity=="medium","audit_flush_timeout_ms_and_fluent_bit_version_skew", true(),"refresh_baselines_in_parser_inventory_csv")
| table _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id flush_timeout_ms emit_ratio stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count severity recommended_action pattern baseline_ratio owner```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_10_fluentbit_multiline_stuck_state_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -6h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_10_fluentbit_multiline_stuck_state | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch imbalance, materialize each arm hourly into a summary index keyed on merge_key, widen alert searches on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap wildcard volume on app:java with production index allowlists after security agrees you are not dropping rare evidence rows.

Dashboard design: host a matrix by cluster and multiline_parser with severity-colored rows, sparklines of emit_ratio, and drilldowns to fluentbit:metrics labels, fluentbit:server_log excerpts, app:java samples, fluentbit:config_snapshot JSON, and Performance host buckets. Optional Splunk ITSI episodes may bind KPIs to counts of critical rows per pipeline_region when your organization models log collection as a service.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the suspect pod. Compare live curl against the Fluent Bit metrics port for fluentbit_multiline_emit_total and fluentbit_input_records_total to the Splunk row for the same fluentbit_input_id inside the last scrape window. When ratios disagree, verify label cardinality, stale pod_name after restarts, and duplicate scrape jobs before blaming parsers.

Cross-check configuration: kubectl describe ConfigMap for the declared multiline_parser name, then read fluentbit:config_snapshot events and confirm parser equals multiline_parser after reload. When kubectl shows a fix but Splunk still shows drift, suspect incomplete hot reload or two ConfigMaps mounted to different containers.

Downstream validation: pick a critical severity row, open the app:java event in Splunk, and confirm stack_truncated and stack_lines_count match manual line counts in the raw field. Pair with sister datacenter traffic: when only one region shows cliffs, diff parser inventory CSV rows and chart versions.

Synthetic validation: in a lab namespace, inject a multiline rule with an impossible end regular expression, confirm observability_uc_13_7_10_fluentbit_multiline_stuck_state returns high within two evaluation windows, restore the correct rule, reload, and confirm severity returns to low.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after merges settle, filter severity to critical and high and expect zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds match your calibrated noise floor.

Runbook linkage: document expected ratio depression during single-line-only deploy windows so validators do not file false defects when change calendars show an approved rollout.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — fluentbit_multiline_emit_total flatlines while fluentbit_input_records_total climbs on one fluentbit_input_id: open fluentbit:server_log multiline lines for buffer timeout language, diff fluentbit:config_snapshot end-regex against live application log samples, and roll a corrected parser under change control.

Case 2 — Emissions cluster on flush_timeout_ms boundaries: capture scrape timestamps modulo timeout, confirm stuck-state ejection rather than natural end-of-stack matches, tune rules or raise timeout only after verifying application log format stability.

Case 3 — Java stacks truncate after NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0 while peer clusters show full depth: compare fluentbit_multiline_parser_inventory.csv pattern_type rows, verify CRI versus docker mode, and reconcile Helm values that set the wrong multiline_parser name.

Case 4 — Python traceback cliff drops on stack_lines_count median: align multiline continuation detection with indented lines, validate stderr versus stdout pipelines, and ensure JSON parsers are not stripping leading spaces.

Case 5 — multiline_parser field on events disagrees with parser in config_snapshot: force a controlled DaemonSet restart to clear partial reload state, verify only one ConfigMap generation is mounted, and audit GitOps drift between regions.

Case 6 — Cross-DC truncation ratio 10x in one datacenter: diff chart Fluent Bit versions using inventory fluent_bit_min_version, merge sister_dc_ratio_floor hints, and schedule coordinated upgrades if release notes cite multiline defects.

Case 7 — Hybrid Fluentd node shows concat timeouts while Fluent Bit nodes look healthy: follow Fluentd concat filter documentation for timeout_sec and partial_value patterns without muting this search; keep detections separate per engine.

Case 8 — Downstream Splunk shows unbalanced braces or mid-line stack endings while metrics look nominal: inspect props.conf LINE_BREAKER and TRUNCATE on intermediate forwarders; multiline may be correct at the edge but broken later in the chain.

Case 9 — Per-pod time_since_last_emit_seconds extreme with zero stuck_records_count in logs: increase server log verbosity temporarily on a canary, capture internal buffer counters if exposed, and validate input backpressure unrelated to UC-13.7.6 flush latency narratives.

Case 10 — Performance tstats arm spikes on the same host as ratio collapse: prioritize node CPU steal and noisy neighbor investigation after confirming multiline rules; avoid conflating host pressure with TLS (UC-13.7.9) without error text.

Case 11 — Inventory CSV stale expected_emit_ratio_baseline causes constant medium severity: refresh baselines from thirty-day medians per cluster and parser, document seasonal traffic drops, and automate replication from metrics stores.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms including otel_metrics fluentbit:metrics, ops fluentbit:server_log component=multiline, app app:java multilineerror, ops fluentbit:config_snapshot, and tstats Performance; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists sixteen analyst-facing columns including recommended_action and baseline_ratio.

Extended platform narrative for fleet operators: treat multiline_parser inventory rows as first-class configuration items alongside network policies. When platform engineers promote a new logging chart, require a pull request that updates expected_emit_ratio_baseline using a fourteen-day trailing median from otel_metrics to avoid alert thrash. Document language-specific stack heuristics: Java stacks often begin with Exception class names and continue with at lines; Python tracebacks begin with Traceback and continue with File lines; Go panics differ again. A parser tuned only for Java will mangle Python workloads silently unless separate inputs carry distinct multiline_parser labels. For CRI partial lines, verify that the concat of partial records occurs before JSON parsing when envelopes carry escaped newlines; ordering mistakes surface as valid JSON with truncated message fields rather than obvious parser errors.

Version governance: maintain a rolling fluent_bit_min_version column per cluster in fluentbit_multiline_parser_inventory.csv and fail change pipelines when a region drifts more than two minor versions behind a reference region without a recorded exception. Pair release-note review with this control so multiline defect fixes are not lost in routine image bumps. When Calyptia or vendor distributions lag upstream, annotate inventory notes with distribution-specific identifiers to prevent false confidence during audits.

Downstream Splunk parsing: Universal Forwarder and heavy forwarder configurations can re-break merged records if LINE_BREAKER ignores SHOULD_LINEMERGE settings. When this UC shows healthy agent ratios yet search-time stacks still truncate, move investigation to intermediate tiers and mirror UC evidence in the configuration management database for forwarders. Keep multilineerror field population consistent across applications so the app arm does not silently empty during schema migrations.

Governance overlays: stamp change_ticket_id on config_snapshot events when Helm values change flush_timeout_ms or parser sections. Archive weekly snapshots of fluentbit_pod_inventory.csv to a restricted evidence index for auditors who require ownership traceability. Pair alert outputs with pipeline_region or datacenter labels so large enterprises route pages to regional squads without manual lookup.

Advanced forensics: when ratio_gap oscillates with batch job schedules, separate service identities for cron-driven namespaces using role labels in fluentbit_pod_inventory.csv rather than muting the entire cluster. For serverless-style nodes that appear and disappear quickly, ensure pod_name cardinality in Prometheus labels is bounded; excessive cardinality can stall scrapers and mimic parser stalls.

Capacity footnote: this control intentionally does not optimize search cost. If multisearch runtime exceeds service level objectives, pre-aggregate otel_metrics into fifteen-minute summary indexes keyed on cluster, pod_name, and multiline_parser while retaining raw arms for investigations. Security isolation: restrict index=app access to roles that require stack content; metrics-only roles can retain otel_metrics and ops arms for operational dashboards without reading application payloads.

Collaboration with developers: require service owners to publish sample stack traces whenever they change logging libraries or async exception wrappers. Insert those samples into runbooks linked from fluentbit_multiline_parser_inventory.csv so regex authors regression-test against real strings rather than abbreviated documentation excerpts.

Closing operational guidance: rehearse this saved search quarterly with tabletop exercises that include observability, Kubernetes platform, and representative application leads. Rotate runbook URLs when teams reorganize so pager deep links do not rot. Keep a single source of truth for sister datacenter pairing labels to prevent mistaken cross-region comparisons during partial failovers.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.10 Fluent Bit multiline stuck-state, flush_timeout ejection, parser drift, stack-trace truncation; tunables earliest=-6h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=otel_metrics sourcetype=fluentbit:metrics earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,k8s_cluster_name,eks_cluster,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,pod,kubernetes.pod_name,fluentbit_pod,"unknown_pod"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,kubernetes.node_name,node,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval multiline_parser=trim(toString(coalesce(multiline_parser,parser,mlp_name,"")))
      | eval parser=trim(toString(coalesce(parser,multiline_parser,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_input_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_input_id,input_name,plugin_id,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_output_id,output_name,"")))
      | eval inp_rec=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_input_records_total,fb_input_records,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval inp_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_input_bytes_total,fb_input_bytes,"0")),10)
      | eval ml_emit=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_multiline_emit_total,fb_multiline_emit_total,"0")),10)
      | eval emit_ratio=if(inp_rec>0 AND ml_emit>=0, round(ml_emit/inp_rec,5), null())
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_timeout_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,rule_pattern,"")))
      | eval lane="otel_prom_fluentbit"
      | eval stack_lines_count=null()
      | eval stack_truncated=null()
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=null()
      | eval stuck_records_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count ]
    [ search index=ops sourcetype=fluentbit:server_log component=multiline earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,kubernetes.pod_name,host,"unknown_pod"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,kubernetes.node_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval multiline_parser=trim(toString(coalesce(multiline_parser,parser,mlp,"")))
      | eval parser=multiline_parser
      | eval fluentbit_input_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_input_id,input_id,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_output_id,"")))
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_timeout_ms,timeout_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,regex_tail,"")))
      | eval stuck_hint=if(match(lower(_raw),"timeout|flush|partial|buffer|stuck|incomplete"),1,0)
      | eval inp_rec=tonumber("0",10)
      | eval inp_bytes=tonumber("0",10)
      | eval ml_emit=tonumber("0",10)
      | eval emit_ratio=null()
      | eval lane="fluentbit_server_multiline_log"
      | eval stack_lines_count=null()
      | eval stack_truncated=null()
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(time_since_last_emit_seconds,idle_sec,"0")),10)
      | eval stuck_records_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(stuck_records_count,buffer_stuck,"0")),10)
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count stuck_hint ]
    [ search index=app sourcetype=app:java earliest=-6h@h latest=now multilineerror=*
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,dc,rack,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,k8s_pod,host,"unknown_pod"))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval multiline_parser="downstream_splunk"
      | eval parser=multiline_parser
      | eval fluentbit_input_id="na_downstream"
      | eval fluentbit_output_id="na_downstream"
      | eval inp_rec=0
      | eval inp_bytes=0
      | eval ml_emit=0
      | eval emit_ratio=null()
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=0
      | eval pattern=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(_raw,""))),1,120)
      | eval stack_lines_count=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(stack_lines_count,stack_depth,lines_in_stack,"0")),10)
      | eval stack_truncated=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(stack_truncated,truncated,stack_trunc,"0")),10)
      | eval lane="app_java_downstream_truncation"
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=null()
      | eval stuck_records_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count ]
    [ search index=ops sourcetype=fluentbit:config_snapshot earliest=-6h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,dc,"unknown_cluster"))))
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pod_name,host,"unknown_pod"))))
      | eval host=pod_name
      | eval multiline_parser=trim(toString(coalesce(multiline_parser,declared_mlp,"")))
      | eval parser=trim(toString(coalesce(parser,active_parser,runtime_parser,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_input_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_input_id,"")))
      | eval fluentbit_output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(fluentbit_output_id,"")))
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(flush_timeout_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval pattern=trim(toString(coalesce(pattern,first_line_regex,"")))
      | eval cfg_drift=if(len(multiline_parser)>0 AND len(parser)>0 AND multiline_parser!=parser,1,0)
      | eval inp_rec=0
      | eval inp_bytes=0
      | eval ml_emit=0
      | eval emit_ratio=null()
      | eval lane="fluentbit_config_snapshot"
      | eval stack_lines_count=null()
      | eval stack_truncated=null()
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=null()
      | eval stuck_records_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count cfg_drift ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=false count AS perf_hits FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-6h@h latest=now BY host
      | eval cluster="perf_datamodel_host"
      | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval host=pod_name
      | eval multiline_parser="perf_correlation"
      | eval parser=multiline_parser
      | eval fluentbit_input_id="perf_dm"
      | eval fluentbit_output_id="perf_dm"
      | eval inp_rec=perf_hits
      | eval inp_bytes=0
      | eval ml_emit=0
      | eval emit_ratio=null()
      | eval flush_timeout_ms=0
      | eval pattern="Performance.host_load_correlation"
      | eval lane="cim_performance_tstats"
      | eval stack_lines_count=null()
      | eval stack_truncated=null()
      | eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=null()
      | eval stuck_records_count=null()
      | fields _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id inp_rec inp_bytes ml_emit emit_ratio flush_timeout_ms pattern lane stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count ]
| eval merge_key=strcat(cluster,"|",pod_name,"|",coalesce(multiline_parser,"__none__"))
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=24 current=t global=f last(ml_emit) AS prev_ml last(inp_rec) AS prev_inp last(_time) AS prev_ts BY merge_key
| eval time_since_last_emit_seconds=coalesce(time_since_last_emit_seconds,if(isnotnull(prev_ts),round(_time-prev_ts,2),null()))
| eval emit_ratio_roll=if(isnotnull(emit_ratio),emit_ratio,if(prev_inp>0 AND prev_ml>=0,round(prev_ml/max(prev_inp,1),5),null())
| eventstats median(emit_ratio) AS dc_emit_p50 stdev(emit_ratio) AS dc_emit_sd BY cluster multiline_parser
| eventstats median(stack_lines_count) AS stack_p50 stdev(stack_lines_count) AS stack_sd BY cluster
| join type=left max=0 cluster pod_name
    [| inputlookup fluentbit_pod_inventory.csv
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | eval pod_name=lower(trim(toString(pod_name)))
     | fields cluster pod_name role owner expected_baseline owner_team_lookup ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster multiline_parser
    [| inputlookup fluentbit_multiline_parser_inventory.csv
     | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(cluster)))
     | eval multiline_parser=trim(toString(parser_id))
     | fields cluster multiline_parser pattern_type expected_emit_ratio_baseline sister_dc_ratio_floor fluent_bit_min_version ]
| eval baseline_ratio=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(expected_emit_ratio_baseline,expected_baseline,"0.02")),10)
| eval ratio_gap=if(isnotnull(emit_ratio) AND baseline_ratio>0, round(emit_ratio/baseline_ratio,4), null())
| eval trunc_cliff=if(stack_truncated==1 AND isnotnull(stack_p50) AND stack_lines_count<stack_p50*0.35,1,0)
| eval severity=case(trunc_cliff==1 AND match(lower(pattern),"nativemethodaccessor|traceback|caused by"),"critical", ratio_gap>0 AND ratio_gap<0.2 AND inp_rec>500,"high", coalesce(cfg_drift,0)==1,"high", coalesce(stuck_records_count,0)>=3 OR time_since_last_emit_seconds>480,"high", stack_truncated==1,"medium", dc_emit_sd>0 AND emit_ratio<dc_emit_p50*0.5,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","rewire_multiline_rule_and_validate_java_python_tail_in_splunk_sample", severity=="high","diff_config_snapshot_reload_hot_and_compare_sister_dc_inventory", severity=="medium","audit_flush_timeout_ms_and_fluent_bit_version_skew", true(),"refresh_baselines_in_parser_inventory_csv")
| table _time cluster pod_name host multiline_parser fluentbit_input_id fluentbit_output_id flush_timeout_ms emit_ratio stack_lines_count stack_truncated time_since_last_emit_seconds stuck_records_count severity recommended_action pattern baseline_ratio owner
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_pct) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance.Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS perf_host
| eval perf_host=lower(trim(toString(perf_host)))
| table perf_host cpu_load mem_used
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by cluster and multiline_parser with drilldowns to fluentbit:metrics, fluentbit:server_log, app:java samples, fluentbit:config_snapshot JSON, and Performance tstats rows; single-value tiles for ratio_gap; timeline of time_since_last_emit_seconds.

## Known False Positives

Single-line application bursts during deploy windows can depress multiline emit ratios until the first real exception arrives; require dwell thresholds and change_ticket correlation before paging owners. Intentionally short flush_timeout_ms on low-latency tracing pipelines produces timeout-driven emissions that are policy-approved; read fluentbit_multiline_parser_inventory.csv notes before treating as incidents. Planned log-format migrations change start and end regular expressions; expect temporary ratio volatility until baselines refresh. Container restarts wipe in-flight buffers and can look like sudden truncation cliffs; correlate with Kubernetes restart counts. Debug-only multiline patterns during incident triage inflate server log noise; tag canary pods separately. Sandbox and development pods with minimal traffic produce sparse ratios; use cluster tier labels to downgrade severity. One-shot CronJob pods may exit before any multiline match completes; exclude job patterns in lookup CSVs when governance agrees. Duplicate Prometheus scrapes can double counters and distort ratios until dedupe rules land. Stale inventory expected_emit_ratio_baseline rows after seasonal traffic shifts cause benign medium severities until CSV automation updates. Sister datacenter comparisons misfire when one region runs a different logging chart version intentionally; verify sister_dc_ratio_floor annotations.
